content:
# IVX OWNER AI — P0 RECOVERY, FULL CONNECTION AUDIT, DEPLOYMENT AND VERIFICATION

MODE: EXECUTE NOW
PRIORITY: P0 CRITICAL
SCOPE: IVX Owner AI chat, AI gateway, execution guard, developer worker, tools, routes, ports, providers, database, deployment and production verification.

DO NOT RETURN A PLAN ONLY.
DO NOT RETURN NARRATIVE CLAIMS.
DO NOT MARK VERIFIED WITHOUT REAL TEST EVIDENCE.
DO NOT ASK FOR CREDENTIALS UNLESS A REAL runtime request returns missing, 401, 403, expired or revoked.
Assume credentials already exist in protected runtime variables and verify their loading without exposing secret values.

==================================================
CURRENT CONFIRMED FAILURES
==================================================

FAILURE 1 — AI PROVIDER / MODEL ADAPTER MISMATCH

Observed production error:

IVXAIGatewayRequestError
endpoint=https://api.openai.com/v1/gpt-4o
model=gpt-4o
providerError=Unsupported model version v4 for provider "openai.responses"
AI SDK 5 only supports models implementing specification version "v2"
Origin:
/app/backend/ivx-ai-runtime.ts:250:22

This is not an owner-message problem.
This is an incorrect provider/model/API adapter configuration.

FAILURE 2 — DEVELOPER EXECUTION GUARD BLOCKING VALID OUTPUT

Observed result:

Development-task response blocked by execution guard.
missing required sections:
TASK UNDERSTOOD
FILES INSPECTED
FILES CHANGED
COMMANDS RUN
TEST RESULT
TYPECHECK RESULT
STATUS
PROOF

The guard also rejected normal phrases such as:
"starting implementation"
"I will inspect"
"and return only files changed"

This means the enforcement layer is rejecting the developer worker before execution and is preventing commands, tests and code changes.

==================================================
PHASE 1 — FREEZE AND CAPTURE BASELINE
==================================================

Before changing code:

1. Record:
   - active Git branch
   - repository HEAD SHA
   - deployed Render service
   - active Render deployment ID
   - production boot time
   - backend version
   - Expo/app version
   - AI SDK version
   - OpenAI SDK/provider package versions
   - runtime Node/Bun version

2. Save the exact existing errors and stack traces.

3. Identify every file involved in:
   - IVX Owner AI message handling
   - AI provider selection
   - model selection
   - OpenAI Responses API
   - OpenAI Chat Completions API
   - gateway normalization
   - fallback provider logic
   - developer execution worker
   - execution guard
   - response schema validation
   - tool execution
   - proof ledger
   - chat persistence

Likely files include but are not limited to:
- backend/ivx-ai-runtime.ts
- backend AI gateway files
- model-provider configuration
- developer execution guard
- owner-room handler
- chat route/controller
- AI worker/tool runner
- environment configuration
- Render start configuration

Do not stop after inspecting one file.

==================================================
PHASE 2 — FIX OPENAI PROVIDER AND MODEL ROUTING
==================================================

1. Audit all OpenAI provider initialization code.

2. Find why the application is combining:
   - provider: openai.responses
   - model: gpt-4o
   - endpoint: /v1/gpt-4o
   - unsupported specification version v4

3. Remove any manually constructed invalid model endpoint such as:
   https://api.openai.com/v1/gpt-4o

4. Use one supported API integration consistently:
   - official Responses API through the compatible provider adapter, or
   - official Chat Completions API through the compatible adapter.

5. Do not mix:
   - an AI SDK v5 provider adapter
   - an incompatible legacy model object
   - a custom endpoint pretending the model name is an API route.

6. Verify the model identifier through configuration rather than embedding it into the endpoint path.

7. Audit and align package versions:
   - ai
   - @ai-sdk/openai or equivalent OpenAI adapter
   - openai
   - any gateway package
   - all transitive provider adapters

8. Remove duplicate or conflicting AI SDK versions from the lockfile.

9. Ensure only one supported provider factory is used by the IVX Owner AI runtime.

10. Add startup validation that checks:
    - provider exists
    - model exists
    - API key variable is bound
    - API base URL is valid
    - provider/model adapter versions are compatible

11. The startup validation must expose only safe metadata:
    - provider name
    - model name
    - adapter version
    - key loaded: true/false
    - never expose the key value.

12. Add controlled fallback behavior:
    - primary supported OpenAI model
    - one explicitly configured fallback model
    - no silent switch to an incompatible provider
    - return a trace ID when all providers fail.

13. Normalize provider failures into:
    - provider
    - model
    - endpoint family
    - HTTP status
    - error code
    - trace ID
    - retryable true/false

14. Never show raw internal stack traces or sensitive configuration in the normal owner chat UI.
    Keep full stack traces in secured server logs.

==================================================
PHASE 3 — FIX THE EXECUTION GUARD
==================================================

The execution guard must validate completed execution reports, not block execution before it starts.

1. Trace the complete flow:

owner message
→ task classification
→ developer worker
→ tool authorization
→ repository inspection
→ code change
→ commands
→ tests
→ report formatter
→ execution guard
→ final response

2. Confirm where the guard currently runs.

3. Move final-report validation after actual execution.

4. Do not require final proof fields before the worker has had the opportunity to inspect files and run commands.

5. Replace fragile narrative phrase detection with a structured result schema.

6. Use a structured internal result object similar to:

{
  "taskUnderstood": "...",
  "filesInspected": [],
  "filesChanged": [],
  "commandsRun": [],
  "testResult": {
    "status": "PASS|FAIL|NOT_RUN",
    "details": []
  },
  "typecheckResult": {
    "status": "PASS|FAIL|NOT_RUN",
    "details": []
  },
  "status": "VERIFIED|FAILED|BLOCKED",
  "proof": {
    "commitSha": null,
    "deployId": null,
    "healthStatus": null,
    "traceIds": []
  }
}

7. Validate the object schema after the worker finishes.

8. Do not reject harmless text such as:
   - starting implementation
   - inspecting files
   - running tests
   - returning changed files

9. Only block when:
   - the user is unauthorized
   - the requested action is unsafe
   - required tools are genuinely unavailable
   - runtime credentials are proven missing/invalid
   - repository write access is denied
   - deployment authorization is denied
   - output falsely claims proof that does not exist

10. Add explicit execution states:
    - RECEIVED
    - AUTHORIZED
    - INSPECTING
    - PATCHING
    - TESTING
    - COMMITTING
    - DEPLOYING
    - VERIFYING
    - VERIFIED
    - FAILED
    - BLOCKED

11. The “Live Work” panel must display these real states from the backend worker, not simulated text.

12. Every state transition must include:
    - timestamp
    - task ID
    - trace ID
    - current operation
    - success/failure
    - safe error summary

==================================================
PHASE 4 — OWNER AI CHAT RECOVERY
==================================================

Test and repair every chat path:

1. New conversation.
2. Existing conversation.
3. Plain text.
4. Long text.
5. Code block.
6. Screenshot upload.
7. Multiple images.
8. PDF upload.
9. Video metadata.
10. Reply.
11. Pin.
12. Copy.
13. Message retry.
14. Network disconnect.
15. Network reconnect.
16. App background/resume.
17. App restart.
18. Render restart.
19. Duplicate-send prevention.
20. Message ordering.
21. Persistent message IDs.
22. Scroll-to-latest.
23. Keyboard behavior.
24. Attachment preview.
25. Upload progress.
26. Upload failure and retry.
27. AI response timeout.
28. Provider fallback.
29. Tool execution response.
30. Developer report rendering.

Acceptance requirements:

- No provider-version error.
- No invalid model endpoint.
- No infinite loading.
- No duplicate messages.
- No disappearing messages.
- No out-of-order messages.
- No fake “working” status.
- No success message before the server confirms success.
- Every failed request receives a trace ID.
- Every message persists after app and server restart.

==================================================
PHASE 5 — DEVELOPER CAPABILITY AUDIT
==================================================

Test each capability independently.

A. REPOSITORY
- read repository
- list files
- inspect file
- search code
- create branch
- modify file
- generate diff
- revert change
- commit
- push
- read latest SHA

B. COMMAND EXECUTION
- run safe shell command
- install dependencies
- lint
- typecheck
- unit test
- integration test
- production build

C. DATABASE
- load Supabase runtime binding
- safe health query
- read members count
- read investors count
- read conversations
- write one controlled QA record
- read it back
- delete the controlled QA record
- verify realtime connection

D. DEPLOYMENT
- identify correct repository/service binding
- confirm branch
- trigger deployment
- capture deployment ID
- capture deployed commit SHA
- verify build logs
- verify runtime health
- verify production commit equals repository HEAD

E. MOBILE
- Expo configuration validation
- Android build validation
- iOS configuration validation
- environment loading
- API URL resolution
- production owner-room request

F. SECURITY
- owner authorization
- admin authorization
- member denial
- expired token
- invalid token
- session refresh
- no secret leakage
- destructive operations remain owner-gated

==================================================
PHASE 6 — ROUTE, ROOT, PORT AND CONNECTION AUDIT
==================================================

Audit every connection used by IVX Owner AI.

1. Frontend route to owner chat.
2. Frontend API base URL.
3. Owner-room API route.
4. AI gateway route.
5. Developer-worker route.
6. Tool-execution route.
7. Upload route.
8. WebSocket/realtime route.
9. Supabase REST connection.
10. Supabase realtime connection.
11. GitHub API connection.
12. Render API/service connection.
13. Production health route.
14. Internal callback routes.
15. CORS configuration.
16. Authentication middleware.
17. Owner-role middleware.
18. Reverse proxy.
19. Render exposed port.
20. Application listen port.
21. Host binding must accept production traffic.
22. Timeout settings.
23. Request size limits.
24. Attachment size limits.
25. Rate limits.
26. Retry policy.
27. DNS target.
28. TLS.
29. API domain.
30. Chat domain.
31. Landing domain.
32. Mobile production API URL.
33. Expo development API URL.
34. Android network-security behavior.
35. iOS transport-security behavior.

For each route or connection report:
- source
- destination
- environment
- method
- expected status
- actual status
- latency
- authentication result
- trace ID
- PASS/FAIL

Do not invent “1,000 roots.”
Discover the actual application routes and dependencies from the repository and runtime configuration, then test every discovered item.

==================================================
PHASE 7 — AUTOMATED TESTS TO ADD
==================================================

Add regression tests for the exact failures.

1. Provider/model compatibility test.
2. Invalid model endpoint test.
3. Provider initialization test.
4. Runtime key-binding test.
5. Gateway normalization test.
6. Developer guard does not run before execution.
7. Completed structured report passes validation.
8. Unsupported report fails with clear reason.
9. Tool execution states appear in correct order.
10. Chat message persists.
11. Retry does not duplicate.
12. Attachment upload succeeds.
13. Provider failure returns trace ID.
14. Fallback model works.
15. Owner authorization works.
16. Non-owner developer execution is denied.
17. Health endpoint returns deployed SHA.
18. Deployment SHA matches repository SHA.

==================================================
PHASE 8 — REQUIRED COMMANDS
==================================================

Run the repository’s real commands after inspecting package configuration.

At minimum execute the applicable equivalents of:

- dependency install with locked versions
- dependency tree inspection
- duplicate package/version inspection
- lint
- typecheck
- unit tests
- integration tests
- production build
- backend startup smoke test
- owner-room API smoke test
- AI provider smoke test
- developer-worker smoke test

Do not claim a command ran unless its real exit code and output were captured.

==================================================
PHASE 9 — DEPLOY
==================================================

After all P0 issues are fixed:

1. Commit the changes to the correct owner-controlled repository.
2. Push to the correct production branch.
3. Trigger or confirm the Render production deployment.
4. Wait for the deployment to complete.
5. Confirm the deployed SHA.
6. Confirm the health endpoint.
7. Send a real test message through production IVX Owner AI.
8. Execute one controlled developer task:
   - inspect one harmless file
   - make no destructive change
   - run one real validation command
   - return structured proof
9. Upload one screenshot.
10. Confirm the AI can analyze the attachment without crashing.
11. Restart/reload and confirm message persistence.

==================================================
PHASE 10 — RELEASE GATES
==================================================

Do not mark VERIFIED unless all are true:

□ Owner AI sends and receives messages.
□ OpenAI provider responds without adapter/version errors.
□ Execution guard allows real work.
□ Developer worker inspects repository files.
□ Commands execute.
□ Tests execute.
□ Typecheck executes.
□ Structured report renders correctly.
□ Chat history persists.
□ Attachments work.
□ GitHub commit exists.
□ Production deployment succeeded.
□ Deployed SHA matches committed SHA.
□ Production health returns success.
□ Production owner-room smoke test passes.
□ No P0 or P1 issue remains.

==================================================
MANDATORY FINAL RESPONSE FORMAT
==================================================

TASK UNDERSTOOD:
[exact scope completed]

ROOT CAUSES:
1. [confirmed root cause]
2. [confirmed root cause]
3. [additional confirmed causes]

FILES INSPECTED:
- [real path]
- [real path]

FILES CHANGED:
- [real path + exact change]
- [real path + exact change]

DEPENDENCIES CHANGED:
- [package old version → new version]
- [lockfile result]

ROUTES AND CONNECTIONS TESTED:
- [route/source/destination/status]
- [route/source/destination/status]

COMMANDS RUN:
- [command]
  Exit code:
  Result:

TEST RESULT:
- Unit:
- Integration:
- Owner chat:
- Attachment:
- Provider:
- Developer worker:
- Persistence:
- Authorization:

TYPECHECK RESULT:
- PASS/FAIL
- Error count:

BUILD RESULT:
- PASS/FAIL
- Build ID:
- Duration:

DATABASE RESULT:
- Supabase connection:
- Read test:
- Controlled write/read/delete test:
- Realtime:

GITHUB PROOF:
- Repository:
- Branch:
- Commit SHA:
- Commit URL or internal reference:
- Push timestamp:

DEPLOYMENT PROOF:
- Render service:
- Deploy ID:
- Deployment status:
- Deployed SHA:
- Deployment timestamp:
- Boot time:

PRODUCTION PROOF:
- Production owner-room URL:
- Health URL:
- HTTP status:
- Response SHA:
- Provider/model:
- Test message ID:
- AI response ID:
- Trace ID:
- Attachment test ID:

BUGS FIXED:
1. [bug and evidence]
2. [bug and evidence]

REMAINING ISSUES:
- [real issue, or NONE]

STATUS:
VERIFIED / FAILED / BLOCKED

RULE:
Use VERIFIED only when GitHub SHA, deployment ID, health response, live owner-chat message and developer-worker execution evidence are all present.
